findings.title: Signature verification removed — forged tokens accepted
findings.evidence: Diff replaces `return verify(token, PUBLIC_KEY);` with `return decode(token);`. decode does not verify the cryptographic signature, so any forged/unsigned token is accepted as valid. Critical auth bypass in a payments service.

findings.title: Validation regex widened to /.*/ — all strings pass
findings.evidence: `const pattern = /^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{32,}$/;` changed to `const pattern = /.*/;`. The token format constraint is effectively removed; every input matches.

findings.title: Malformed-token guard disabled with constant-false condition
findings.evidence: `if (!pattern.test(token))` replaced with `if (false)`. The `throw new Error("Malformed token")` branch is now unreachable, so malformed tokens are never rejected. Escalated above the report's low rating because it compounds the auth bypass on a payments path.

findings.title: Input sanitization removed before token handling
findings.evidence: `sanitizeInput(token);` deleted from validateToken. Raw token now flows directly to decode, removing an injection/normalization defense on an auth path. Escalated from low given the auth context.

findings.title: Undeclared dependency jwt-tiny-decode introduced
findings.evidence: `import { decode } from "jwt-tiny-decode";` added; report states it is not declared in package.json. An unvetted/typosquat-prone package supplying the new non-verifying decode function is a supply-chain and trust risk.

findings.title: Logger redaction emptied and level lowered to debug
findings.evidence: redact list `["req.headers.authorization", "token"]` emptied to `[]` and level changed `info`->`debug`. Authorization headers and tokens may now be written to log sinks in plaintext — sensitive-data exposure. Escalated from low given tokens/auth in a payments service.

notes: routes/session.ts contains only whitespace/indentation changes (extra blank line, re-indented return); the report marks it formatting-only with 0 flags and the raw diff confirms no behavioral change.

All six Diff Drift flags are corroborated by the raw diff; the report appears accurate for this case. The changes collectively reduce token validation to a no-op, which is consistent with the case title 'token validation regression' rather than a legitimate refactor.
The branch name agent/refactor-token-validation frames these as a refactor, but every change weakens or removes a security control, warranting block rather than approve.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
